name: Electra
description: Electra, in Greek mythology, is a pivotal figure whose life is intertwined with themes of vengeance, justice, and familial loyalty. She was the daughter of King Agamemnon of Mycenae and Queen Clytemnestra. While Agamemnon was away fighting in the Trojan War, Clytemnestra took Aegisthus as her lover. Upon Agamemnon's triumphant return, Clytemnestra and Aegisthus murdered him. Electra, horrified by this act of treachery, rescued her younger brother, Orestes, sending him away to Phocis to be raised in safety. Electra lived under the oppressive rule of Clytemnestra and Aegisthus, constantly mourning her father's death and yearning for Orestes' return. She nurtured a burning desire for revenge against her mother and stepfather. When Orestes finally returned as a young man, Electra recognized him and together they plotted their vengeance. With Electra's unwavering support and encouragement, Orestes ultimately killed Clytemnestra and Aegisthus, avenging their father's murder. This act, while fulfilling Electra's desire, also brought about its own set of consequences, as Orestes was then pursued by the Furies for the matricide. Electra's story is a powerful exploration of grief, loyalty, and the cyclical nature of violence. She is a symbol of unwavering devotion to family and the pursuit of justice, even in the face of overwhelming odds. Her character has inspired numerous tragedies and adaptations throughout history, solidifying her place as a compelling and enduring figure in Greek mythology.
subtitle: Avenger of Agamemnon, sister of Orestes
achievements: Inspiring Orestes to avenge their father
Enduring years of oppression under Clytemnestra
Recognizing Orestes after his long absence
Successfully plotting the downfall of Clytemnestra and Aegisthus

keyMyths.title: The Murder of Agamemnon
keyMyths.description: Agamemnon's murder by Clytemnestra and Aegisthus sets the stage for Electra's lifelong quest for vengeance. This act of betrayal fuels her desire for justice and shapes her entire destiny.

keyMyths.title: The Return of Orestes
keyMyths.description: Orestes' return marks a turning point, fulfilling Electra's hopes and providing the means to enact revenge. Their reunion is a powerful moment of recognition and shared purpose.

keyMyths.title: The Vengeance
keyMyths.description: The killing of Clytemnestra and Aegisthus fulfills Electra's desire for revenge but also unleashes further consequences. This act highlights the complex and often tragic nature of justice.

sources.author: Aeschylus
sources.description: A tragic play detailing Agamemnon's murder and foreshadowing the events that follow.
sources.text: A tragic play detailing Agamemnon's murder and foreshadowing the events that follow.

sources.author: Sophocles
sources.description: A play focusing on Electra's unwavering grief and her role in Orestes' return.
sources.text: A play focusing on Electra's unwavering grief and her role in Orestes' return.

sources.author: Euripides
sources.description: A play exploring the psychological complexities of Electra and the consequences of revenge.
sources.text: A play exploring the psychological complexities of Electra and the consequences of revenge.
